reasoning: The World Health Organization (WHO) rarely declares pandemics; the last declarations were H1N1 in 2009 and COVID-19 in 2020. This indicates an annual base rate around 5-10%. As of the knowledge cutoff date (October 26, 2025), there have been no disease outbreaks declared a pandemic by WHO in 2025. A public health emergency of international concern (PHEIC) for mpox was even wound down by the WHO in September 2025. Since there are just over two months remaining in 2025, and no active outbreak is currently being seriously considered for a pandemic declaration, the probability for one occurring before December 31, 2025, is extremely low. Thus, the minimum allowed probability of 0.02 is the most appropriate estimate.
